registry_note: Static first-party capability directory. Records are documentation and policy metadata only; no execution.

capabilities.summary: Read-only Hermes CLI and sanitized config snapshot for operator diagnostics.
capabilities.description: Maps to GET /api/hermes-runtime/inventory. Does not install skills or mutate Hermes home.

provenance.note: Vendored registry entry; no external download.

notes: Execution is limited to Hermes inventory builder; not invoked from directory rows.

risks: Remote-only deployments may return degraded inventory; see HAM_HERMES_SKILLS_MODE.
evidence_expectations: Operators compare inventory JSON to expected Hermes home layout.

capabilities.summary: Join vendored catalog with live hermes skills list output.
capabilities.description: Maps to GET /api/hermes-skills/installed. Read-only observation; install remains separate token-gated flow.

provenance.note: Vendored registry entry; no external download.

notes: Uses fixed hermes subcommand suffix for list; see hermes_skills_live.

risks: CLI absent or remote_only mode yields empty or partial overlay.
evidence_expectations: Overlay rows should align with catalog ids where installed.

bundles.summary: Operator bundle for Hermes runtime visibility (inventory + hub entry).
bundles.description: Documents the read-only path for Hermes local/runtime inspection via HAM API and Hermes Hub UI. No mutations from this bundle.

notes: Does not invoke packaging or shell beyond existing inventory module boundaries.
Unrelated to chat model selection; inventory is non-LLM.

risks: Confusing inventory absence with skills catalog absence.
evidence_expectations: Successful GET /api/hermes-runtime/inventory with expected kind ham_hermes_runtime_inventory.

bundles.summary: Catalog plus live installed skills observation for Hermes runtime.
bundles.description: Covers GET /api/hermes-skills/catalog and GET /api/hermes-skills/installed. Install preview/apply exist elsewhere and are not part of this bundle.

notes: Mutating installs use HAM_SKILLS_WRITE_TOKEN; never triggered by directory APIs in Phase 1.

risks: Operators may assume overlay implies install; document separation clearly.
evidence_expectations: Catalog count stable; overlay reflects CLI when local mode enabled.

bundles.summary: Managed cloud agent launch, status sync, preview card, and chat handoff flows.
bundles.description: Documents Cursor Cloud Agent integration via existing HAM APIs (cursor settings, managed missions, chat). Launch and mutating actions use Cursor API and existing tokens only; this directory row does not execute launches.

notes: Mutability is limited to user-driven flows in UI/API that already enforce Cursor API key and mission handling.
Cloud agent model selection follows Cursor API; not HAM DEFAULT_MODEL.
Chat and handoff state follow existing frontend persistence patterns.

risks: Billing and model tier are Cursor-side; operators should verify Cursor usage.
Confusion between HAM chat gateway model and Cursor cloud agent model.
evidence_expectations: Control plane runs or activity logs reflect cloud agent launches where instrumented.

bundles.summary: HAM CLI doctor/status and desktop packaging workflow documentation.
bundles.description: Checklist-oriented bundle for operators: HAM CLI packaging helpers and Electron shell; does not run pack from this API. Read-only verification via existing CLI and docs.

notes: Packaging runs on developer machine via npm/electron-builder; not invoked by directory.

risks: Operators may expect one-click release from API; clarify manual pack workflow.
evidence_expectations: Successful local CLI runs and published desktop artifacts per desktop/README.

profile_templates.summary: Reference mapping for Agent Builder primary profile without persisting changes.
profile_templates.description: Illustrates how future bundles could map to HamAgentProfile fields. Phase 1 does not apply templates to .ham/settings.json.

notes: Profile edits use separate settings preview/apply with HAM_SETTINGS_WRITE_TOKEN.
Model tier follows models catalog and env.
memory_heist assembles repo truth for chat.

risks: Future phases must not break HamAgentProfile validation when mapping templates.
evidence_expectations: Agent profile changes remain auditable via settings write backups when applied outside directory.
